extraction.one_line_summary: BEVIN Paddy launched a brutally effective sprint with 400m remaining after a rolling stage, capitalizing on TAARAMÄE's late solo catch.
tactical_patterns: VERONA attacked on flat sections of the final climb (2km from top), not the steepest gradient, sparking counterattacks.
Israel Premier Tech planted HAMILTONS in break for KOM points while saving BEVIN, revealing multi-objective breakaway strategy.
TAARAMÄE's late descent attack (post-climb) caught with 500m left, suggesting insufficient gap-building on technical descents before flat finishes.
Jumbo-Visma controlled all day for DENNIS despite only 14-second lead, indicating defensive posture before queen stage.

rider_signals.BEVIN Paddy: positive: explosive sprint after hilly stage moved him to 14 seconds off GC lead, showing versatility beyond expected range
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: second place in reduced sprint after multiple climbs confirms climbing form improvement beyond pure sprint stages
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: third place finish while defending leader's jersey reveals unexpected sprint capacity for time trialist
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: neutral: attacked final climb but couldn't sustain, telegraphing form level before queen stage
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexandr: neutral: followed MÄDER but lacked finishing power, suggesting buildup phase rather than peak form
